key_insight: Prototype-guided latent alignment improves fine-tuning of pretrained MLIPs by anchoring target atoms to similar source-domain chemical environments, boosting generalization in low-data settings.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to adapt foundation-model interatomic potentials to new materials chemistry with fewer DFT labels, reducing the cost of deployment for materials discovery workflows.
method: Cluster atoms by latent representations to form source-domain prototypes for analogous local chemical environments.
Align each target atom’s energy contribution to the nearest source prototype during fine-tuning.
Use the alignment as an inductive bias to preserve pretrained interactions while adapting to out-of-distribution targets.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on rMD17 with MACE and SchNet across multiple data budgets, and on MACE-OFF foundation models on SPICE; the method reduced energy MAE by up to 18% versus standard fine-tuning.
watch_item: Prototype quality and latent-space matching may be sensitive to representation choice and dataset shift, so alignment could underperform if source and target chemistries are too dissimilar.
paper_title: Prototype-Guided Latent Alignment for Data-Efficient Fine-Tuning of Molecular Foundation Models
